resources.name: AI for Education — Student Library
resources.description: Prompts specifically designed for students. Works across Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity.
resources.notes: Probably the closest fit for academic use. Check here first.

resources.name: Northeastern — Claude Learning Mode Guide
resources.description: University-produced guide on using Claude for studying with Calculus-specific examples.
resources.notes: More about how to use Claude than a prompt dump, but the framing is solid.

resources.name: langgptai/awesome-claude-prompts
resources.notes: Includes the "Mr. Ranedeer" AI tutor prompt.

resources.name: Anthropic — Prompt Engineering Overview
resources.description: Entry point to Anthropic's official prompt engineering docs.
resources.notes: Start here for the big picture before diving into specific techniques.

resources.name: Anthropic — Prompting Best Practices
resources.notes: Read this before going deep on prompt collecting — it'll improve your own prompt-writing more than any library.

resources.name: Field Guide to AI — Prompt Library
resources.notes: More business-leaning but has education sections.

resources.name: prompts.chat
resources.description: Community-submitted prompt directory, 1600+ prompts, CC0 licensed.
resources.notes: High volume, variable quality. Has Students / Teaching / Tutoring / Claude tags worth filtering by. Expect to dig — signal-to-noise is mixed.

resources.name: Prompt Advance — Claude Prompts
resources.notes: Business/marketing/SEO-heavy, generic "Act as expert" style. Has a Learning section with Students content but overall skews low-signal. Lower priority than AI for Education or the awesome-claude-prompts GitHub repo.

resources.name: God of Prompt
resources.notes: Heavy on business/marketing/SEO, paid bundles. Skip.
